cmd/sink-worker.purpose: Binary entry point for the Kafka-to-ClickHouse sink pipeline: consumes raw ingest CloudEvents from Kafka, deduplicates, batch-inserts into ClickHouse via streaming.Connector, and publishes EventBatchedIngest flush notifications. Contains only Wire wiring and startup — all sink logic lives in openmeter/sink.

patterns.name: Wire-generated DI via initializeApplication
patterns.description: wire.go (//go:build wireinject) declares the Application struct and wire.Build over common.* provider sets; wire_gen.go is generated and never hand-edited. Add deps by adding an Application field plus a provider reference.
patterns.example: wire.Build(metadata, common.Sink, common.Streaming, common.ClickHouse, common.WatermillNoPublisher, wire.Struct(new(Application), "*"))

patterns.name: oklog/run.Group with reverse shutdown order
patterns.description: main.go adds exactly three actors (Sink.Run/Close, TelemetryServer.ListenAndServe/Shutdown, run.SignalHandler) and calls group.Run(run.WithReverseShutdownOrder()) so Sink stops before telemetry.
patterns.example: group.Add(func() error { return app.Sink.Run(ctx) }, func(err error) { _ = app.Sink.Close() }); err = group.Run(run.WithReverseShutdownOrder())

patterns.name: Viper config load + Validate() before Wire init
patterns.description: Config is loaded via v.Unmarshal into config.Configuration then conf.Validate() runs before initializeApplication; never read config after Wire init.
patterns.example: v.Unmarshal(&conf); conf.Validate(); app, cleanup, err := initializeApplication(ctx, conf)

patterns.name: metadata() binary identity for telemetry
patterns.description: Local metadata() calls common.NewMetadata(conf, version, "sink-worker") and is referenced directly in wire.Build to tag OTel telemetry/logs.
patterns.example: func metadata(conf config.Configuration) common.Metadata { return common.NewMetadata(conf, version, "sink-worker") }

patterns.name: WatermillNoPublisher instead of full router
patterns.description: Sink publishes ingest flush events via a dedicated Kafka publisher (NewSinkWorkerPublisher) and uses common.WatermillNoPublisher — not the full system-events router/DLQ stack.
patterns.example: common.WatermillNoPublisher  // in wire.Build — not common.Watermill or common.EventBus

patterns.name: Unconditional cleanup defer even on init error
patterns.description: The cleanup func from initializeApplication is invoked even when err != nil (its reverse-order cleanupN chain handles partial-init teardown).
patterns.example: app, cleanup, err := initializeApplication(ctx, conf); if err != nil { if cleanup != nil { cleanup() }; os.Exit(1) }; defer cleanup()

key_file_guides.role: DI spec: Application struct fields + wire.Build provider list. Edited only when adding/removing app-level dependencies.
key_file_guides.watch_for: //go:build wireinject tag must be present (else duplicate-symbol errors with wire_gen.go). No business logic.

key_file_guides.role: Generated by Wire; contains the real initializeApplication with the ordered cleanup12()...cleanup() chain.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Never hand-edit; cleanup funcs are chained in reverse-construction order and manual edits break the chain silently.

key_file_guides.role: Entry point: config loading, Wire init, run group assembly, signal handling. No domain logic.
key_file_guides.watch_for: app.SetGlobals() must run before any logging; run.SignalHandler must be the last actor added.

key_file_guides.role: version/revision/revisionDate globals populated by ldflags and debug.BuildInfo; shared across cmd binaries.
key_file_guides.watch_for: //nolint:gochecknoglobals and //nolint:gochecknoinits are intentional — do not remove.

anti_patterns: Adding business logic or domain service calls in main.go or wire.go (logic belongs in openmeter/sink or app/common)
Spawning a goroutine outside run.Group (bypasses graceful shutdown)
Constructing domain services (sink.Sink, streaming.Connector) directly in wire.go instead of via common.* providers
Using context.Background() inside run group actors instead of the root ctx from main()

decisions.decision: WatermillNoPublisher wired instead of a full router
decisions.rationale: Sink only publishes ingest flush events via a dedicated Kafka publisher; it consumes no system events and needs no router/DLQ/retry stack.
SetGlobals() initializes OTel global meter/tracer/propagator in one call so downstream packages are configured before the run group starts.
Single responsibility (Kafka -> ClickHouse) plus a mandatory health/metrics sidecar keeps shutdown ordering simple.

collector/benthos/bloblang.purpose: Side-effect-only plugin package that registers custom Bloblang functions as Benthos/Redpanda Connect plugins via Go init(). Must be blank-imported by the collector binary to activate registrations; exports nothing.

patterns.name: init-based plugin registration
patterns.description: Each custom function is registered inside init() via bloblang.RegisterFunctionV2; no exported symbols — activation is solely by blank import.
patterns.example: func init() { err := bloblang.RegisterFunctionV2("resource_quantity", spec, factory); if err != nil { panic(err) } }

patterns.name: PluginSpec with typed params
patterns.description: Use bloblang.NewPluginSpec() with explicit Param declarations so callers get typed argument extraction via args.GetString/args.GetInt64.

patterns.name: Lazy-closure return
patterns.description: The factory validates/parses arguments once, then returns a func() (any, error) closure for repeated invocation; heavy parsing happens in the factory, not the closure.
patterns.example: return func() (any, error) { return quantity.AsDec().String(), nil }, nil

patterns.name: Empty-value guard before parsing
patterns.description: Check for empty string before delegating to external parsers and return a zero value (not an error) so pipelines tolerate optional fields.
patterns.example: if value == "" { return func() (any, error) { return 0, nil }, nil }

patterns.name: Panic on registration failure
patterns.description: RegisterFunctionV2 errors are fatal — panic immediately (a name collision or bad spec must not be silently ignored).

key_file_guides.role: Registers the 'resource_quantity' Bloblang function converting Kubernetes resource.Quantity strings (e.g. '100m', '2Gi') to decimal via k8s.io/apimachinery resource.ParseQuantity.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Panics on RegisterFunctionV2 error — function names must be globally unique within the binary.

anti_patterns: Exporting functions or types — the package is side-effect-only
Heavy I/O or slow computation inside the returned closure instead of the factory
Returning hard errors for empty/zero input instead of a zero value
Registering the same function name in multiple files (init order is undefined; duplicate name panics)
Skipping the blank import in cmd/benthos-collector — without it nothing registers

decisions.decision: Use init() auto-registration instead of an explicit Register() call
decisions.rationale: Benthos plugins are designed for blank-import activation; a central Register() would couple the launcher to this package's internals.
Kubernetes resource strings have complex SI/binary suffix rules; reusing the authoritative parser guarantees correctness.

collector/benthos/input/runai.purpose: HTTP client library for the Run:ai API providing typed access to workloads, pods, and their metrics via a single resty.Client (service.go) with automatic JWT refresh on every non-token request. Resources follow a strict ListX/ListAllX pagination pair plus chunked metric fetching.

patterns.name: Service method per API endpoint
patterns.description: Every endpoint is a method on *Service: set Accept header, query params via SetQueryParams, call SetResult with a typed pointer, execute the resty verb, then type-assert resp.Result().

patterns.name: ListX + ListAllX pagination pair
patterns.description: Each resource has ListX (single page, max 500 limit) and ListAllX (loops until resp.Next == 0). ListAllX calls s.resourceTypeMetrics.Set after collecting all pages.

patterns.name: Metric chunking at max 9 per request
patterns.description: GetAllWorkloadWithMetrics/GetAllPodWithMetrics chunk MetricType slices with lo.Chunk(..., 9) before GetWorkloadMetrics/GetPodMetrics to stay under the Run:ai undocumented limit.

patterns.name: ResourceWithMetrics interface + MarshalJSON discriminator
patterns.description: WorkloadWithMetrics and PodWithMetrics implement ResourceWithMetrics (GetType, GetMetrics, json.Marshaler); MarshalJSON injects a resourceType field via an anonymous struct embed.

patterns.name: OnBeforeRequest JWT auto-refresh
patterns.description: service.go registers OnBeforeRequest to RefreshToken then SetAuthToken on every non-token request; OnAfterResponse clears the token on 401 and records normalized timing metrics.
patterns.example: if request.URL == "/api/v1/token" { return nil } // skip token endpoint

patterns.name: Non-fatal per-resource metric errors
patterns.description: In GetAllWorkloadWithMetrics/GetAllPodWithMetrics a per-resource metric error is logged and the loop continues (continue) rather than aborting the whole collection.
patterns.example: s.logger.With(...).Errorf("failed to get workload metrics: %w", err); continue

key_file_guides.role: Constructs *Service with a resty.Client and all shared state (logger, client, token, pageSize, resourceTypeMetrics); NewService is the only entry point.
key_file_guides.watch_for: TimingMetrics path normalization uses a regex in OnAfterResponse — add new endpoint path patterns here or metrics will be untagged/high-cardinality.

key_file_guides.role: JWT lifecycle: NewToken posts to /api/v1/token, RefreshToken checks expiry via verifyToken, SetToken/GetToken are field accessors.
key_file_guides.watch_for: verifyToken uses jwt.WithoutClaimsValidation() — it only checks expiry, not signature. Token is cleared on 401 by OnAfterResponse.

key_file_guides.role: MetricType constants (workload and pod scopes), MeasurementParams/Measurement/MeasurementResponse/Metrics, and GetWorkloadMetrics/GetAllWorkloadWithMetrics/GetPodMetrics/GetAllPodWithMetrics.
key_file_guides.watch_for: PodMetricType and WorkloadMetricType share the MetricType string type; some constant names collide across groups — don't assume a constant is scoped to one resource.

key_file_guides.role: Workload struct, ListWorkloads (filterBy excluding non-running phases), ListAllWorkloads.
key_file_guides.watch_for: ListWorkloads also filters client-side by RunningPods > 0 after the API call; including new phases requires editing both filteredOutPhases and the lo.Filter predicate.

key_file_guides.role: Pod struct, ListPods/ListAllPods; ListAllPods calls resourceTypeMetrics.Set(len, "pod") after pagination.
key_file_guides.watch_for: ListPods hardcodes completed=false — only running pods are returned; do not use it to fetch completed pods.

anti_patterns: Adding a resource without both ListX (single page, max 500) and ListAllX (full loop calling resourceTypeMetrics.Set)
Calling GetWorkloadMetrics/GetPodMetrics with more than 9 MetricType values — always chunk with lo.Chunk(..., 9)
Returning an error from the per-resource metric loop instead of log-and-continue
Adding timing metrics without normalizing the URL path via the OnAfterResponse regex
Modifying verifyToken to verify the JWT signature — it is intentionally parsed without signature verification

decisions.decision: Single *Service holds all HTTP state including token and resty.Client
decisions.rationale: The OnBeforeRequest hook closes over *Service so RefreshToken runs transparently before each request without threading credentials through every method.

decisions.decision: Metric fetches chunk at 9 MetricType values per request
decisions.rationale: The Run:ai API has an undocumented max on simultaneous metric types; chunking at 9 stays safely under it while maps.Copy merges partial results.
Lets the Benthos input handle WorkloadWithMetrics and PodWithMetrics polymorphically while producing self-describing JSON with a resourceType field.
